ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-17-2012 Continued To May-10-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 602
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (602)

APPEARANCES:
Bruce Jackson of Keller, Fishback & Jackson representing the plaintiff.
David Mann of Vasquez, Estrada & Conway representing defendant Hills Brothers Chemical Co.
Julie Torrev of DeHay & Elliston representing defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Patrick Callahan of Burnham Brown representing Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.

This matter has been singly assigned to Judge Bolanos for jury trial. The case is on calendar today for a status conference.

At 9:39 a.m., court and counsel confer on the status of settlement discussions. Discussions are still ongoing with the next conference being conducted this Friday (4/20/12) by Judge Jackson. Counsel estimate that the trial will last three (3) weeks.

Judge Bolanos sets the following schedule;
5/3/12: Final settlement deadline
5/4/12: All MILs and contested designations/counter-designations must be filed with the court.
5/8/12: A list of MILs to be ruled upon prior to opening statements must be submitted to Department 602.
5/10/12: Hearing on certain MILs and contested designations/counter-designations.
5/11/12: Hardshipping/Questionnaire Phase of Jury Selection
5/14/12: Voir Dire Phase of Jury Selection

At 10:00 a.m., this matter is adjourned until May 10, 2012 at 9:30 a.m.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Roger Yuen, Esq. of Foley & Mansfield appearing for Defendant Flowserve Corp. and Moving Party / J. Bruce Jackson, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP (818-342-7442) appearing for Plaintiff and Opposing Party

RULING:
Defendant Flowserve Corp. successor-in-interest to Durametallic Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication

Argued and Tentative Ruling Adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Cecil Stephens was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony, where he identified Mr. Stephens' co-workers who had information about Mr. Stephens' work with Durametallic products, precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, as defendant's separate statement did not demonstrate that co-workers did not have such knowledge. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production as to each and every adjudication issue. Prevailing party to prepare order and present order to this court by 6/13/11. JUDGE TERI L. JACKSON, CSR:T. WILLAT-HARRISON, #4609 = (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar May-05-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrotatories, requests for admissions and request for production of documents to defendant Bell Industries, Inc., and plaintiff's request for sanctions.
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, #4780.

Stephen M. Fishback Esq. appeared for plaintiff and moving party David Stephens, individually, and as Successor-In-Interest to Cecil Stephens.
David Y. Parnall, Esq. appeared for defendant Bell Industries, Inc.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court ruled that defendant Bell Industries, Inc. failed to meet & confer are required under the Code of Civil Procedure. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant Bell Industries, Inc. shall pay plaintiff sanctions in the amount of five hundres ($500.00) dollars, payable within ten (10) days from the date of issuance of the Order.

Plaintiff's motion to compel is moot. Plaintiff has received the requested information from defendant.
